learning_skills.description: Learning skills (metacognitive) this game trains. Required per OS spec 2026 Section 5. Pick at least 3.

core_loop_sentence.description: The entire core loop stated in one sentence. If you cannot state it in one sentence, the loop is not clear enough to build.

luck_skill_ratio.description: 0.0 = pure luck, 1.0 = pure skill. Declare explicitly — it shapes difficulty curve and feedback design.

player_type_targets.description: Bartle player types this game is designed for. Pick at most 2. 'Our game is for everyone' is a design failure.

interaction_constraints.description: Explicit declaration of how selection and target-matching work. Required for any game with selectable options and a numeric or positional target.

selection_rule.description: fixed_set_multi_select: each option usable once, disappears when chosen. reusable_multi_select: same option selectable multiple times. single_select: one at a time, replaced each round.

item_reuse_allowed.description: Can the same option be selected more than once in a single round?

selected_items_disappear.description: When an item is selected, does it visually leave the option pool?

target_must_be_solvable.description: Must every generated target be reachable under the current selection_rule? Should almost always be true. False requires explicit justification.

overshoot_recovery.description: blocked: prevent the action. undo_last: remove the offending item. reset_all: clear the slate. none: allow overshoot (only valid for non-exact games).

exact_match_required.description: Must the player reach the target exactly, or is within-tolerance acceptable?

thinking_layer.description: The ninth required design layer (OS spec 2026 Section 3). Defines the metacognitive dimension of the learning loop — what the learner must notice, predict, monitor, and revise. This is the bridge between a good game loop and a good learning loop.

what_to_notice.description: Which features of the presented problem are mathematically relevant? What should the learner look at before acting?

what_to_predict.description: Before selecting or acting, what should the learner mentally compute or estimate? What does the game ask them to anticipate?

what_to_monitor.description: During action, what running state must the learner track? What changes as they act that they must stay aware of?

what_to_revise.description: After feedback, what belief or strategy should change? What is the cleaner version of their attempt?

learning_design.description: Defines how the game detects misconceptions, recognizes understanding, and structures reflection. Required for Approval Gates 3–5 (OS spec 2026 Section 10).

misconception_detection_logic.description: How the game detects and classifies learner errors into the six required error categories. Must be implementable without audio or camera.

error_category_map.description: One entry per detectable error category. Should cover at least 3 of the 6 required categories.

detection_signal.description: Observable, measurable signal the game can log to classify this error.

play_appearance.description: What an observer would see when this error is occurring.

evidence_of_understanding_signals.description: Observable behaviors that indicate the learner has internalized the concept — not just performed correctly once.

guessing_risk_signals.description: Observable behaviors that suggest the player is guessing or pattern-matching rather than reasoning mathematically.

reflection_prompt_plan.description: One prompt for each metacognitive phase. See OS spec 2026 Section 4.2 and Rule D.

adaptation_design.description: Defines how the game responds when different confusion patterns are detected. Adaptation is a distinct design layer — not mere difficulty scaling. See OS spec 2026 Section 4.3 and Approval Gate 4.

hint_trigger_logic.description: What event or pattern triggers a hint, and what the hint shows. e.g. 'after 2 consecutive overshoot events, show running-total label in large font'.

adaptation_trigger_logic.description: What event or pattern triggers a difficulty reduction or representation shift.

alternate_representation_mode.description: Description of the simpler or alternative representation available when the primary one causes confusion.

misconception_specific_review_mode.description: How review mode targets the detected error category specifically. A good retry is not the same level again — it is a cleaner version of the exact misunderstanding.

difficulty_and_adaptation_levers.description: Replaces and extends the concept of a single difficulty axis. Challenge and adaptation must be declared separately — they are independent design dimensions. See OS spec 2026 Section 4.3.

challenge_levers.description: Which pressure axes are active in this game.

adaptation_levers.description: Which adaptation responses are implemented or planned.

transfer_target.description: What later topic, representation, or context this game is preparing the learner for. Required per OS spec 2026 Rule G and Approval Gate 6. e.g. 'Two-digit addition with regrouping; multi-step word problems'.

transfer_echo_design.description: How the next game in the sequence should echo this game's mechanic or representation to support transfer. e.g. 'The grouping mechanic in the multiplication game should use the same running-total display'.

teacher_dashboard_outputs.description: What a teacher can see about student performance. Required for Approval Gate 7. Each item names one visible output. e.g. ['misconception frequency by category', 'students likely guessing', 'accuracy by level'].

validation_targets.description: Measurable targets for the Validation and Measurement block. Defines success criteria beyond 'did the prototype build'.

misconception_capture_rate.description: Target percentage of error events correctly classified by category. e.g. '≥ 70% of impulsive_guess events detected within the session'.

reduction_in_guessing_signals.description: Measurable reduction in impulsive-behavior signals over the course of a session. e.g. 'tap interval < 400ms rate drops by ≥ 20% from first 5 orders to last 5 orders'.

quality_of_transfer_performance.description: How performance on a transfer task is measured. e.g. 'accuracy on two-digit addition problems ≥ 10% higher after Bakery session than before'.

teacher_interpretation_speed.description: Time limit for a teacher to identify the most confused student or most common misconception. e.g. '≤ 60 seconds with the dashboard visible'.

revision_after_feedback_rate.description: Percentage of feedback events that produce a measurable strategy change on the next order. e.g. '≥ 40% of impulsive_guess feedback events followed by longer inter-tap interval'.

approval_gates_2026.description: Explicit pass/fail declaration for each of the ten 2026 approval gates (OS spec 2026 Section 10). A game is not ready unless all ten are true.

difficulty_profile.description: How pressure scales across the game. Level 1 should teach before it pressures.

curve_type.description: smooth_ramp: ideal. spike: dangerous — gate will flag. flat: boring — gate will warn.

intro_pressure_level.description: teaching: Level 1 has no meaningful time/motion pressure. low: minimal pressure. medium: moderate. high: full pressure from Level 1 — gate will revise.

pressure_axes.description: Which pressure types are active simultaneously? Stacking 3+ on Level 1 is a design smell.

level_1_teaching_window_seconds.description: How long does a player have to understand the loop before pressure becomes meaningful? Under 5 seconds on Level 1 is a red flag.

parameters.description: Per-parameter ramp audit thresholds. When present, difficulty_ramp_auditor.py reads these instead of its built-in defaults.

level_1_teaching_min.description: Used when direction=lower_is_harder. Level 1 value must be >= this.

level_1_teaching_max.description: Used when direction=higher_is_harder. Level 1 value must be <= this.

max_step_pct.description: % change above which a transition is flagged as a spike.

min_step_pct.description: % change below which a transition is flagged as flat.

weight.description: Relative importance in weighted severity score. 2.0 = critical.
